footer.tagline: A personal atlas of curiosity, field notes, and strange little breakthroughs that deserve a second look.
footer.exploreHome: Home curates the freshest discoveries from every section.
footer.exploreArchive: Each archive leads to dedicated entries with full reading pages.

hero.description: This journal is part field atlas, part observatory, part studio for the questions that keep glowing after the expedition ends.
hero.imageAlt: Explorer on horseback in a dramatic landscape

homeRail.title: Start with what was published most recently
homeRail.description: This rail is the quickest way into the archive if you want to jump straight to a story instead of browsing by section.

journal.title: Chronicles of wonder
journal.description: A running log of encounters, sketches, and remembered details from the field.

realms.title: Realms and discoveries
realms.description: Every terrain carries a different language. These cards catalogue the places where wonder behaves differently.

experiments.title: Experiments in wonder
experiments.description: Interactive prompts, sideways questions, and playful systems that train a sharper eye.
experiments.fallbackTitle: What changes when the map is also the diary?
experiments.fallbackExcerpt: Use the lab to prototype narrative systems, visual motifs, and curiosity-driven interactions before they become entries.

journal.headline: Field notes from the expedition
journal.description: Essays, sketches, and remembered details from the quiet part of discovery.
journal.readingPathTitle: Move through the journal like a field notebook
journal.readingPathDescription: Each entry reads as a chapter rather than a card in a feed. Start at the top or jump directly into any note below.

realms.headline: Catalogues of strange terrains
realms.description: A visual archive of places, climates, and symbolic ecosystems worth returning to.
realms.atlasDescription: Realms are browsed like destinations. Each card leads to its own detailed record with image, context, and related entries.

experiments.headline: Prompts, systems, and curiosity tests
experiments.description: A laboratory for ideas that need to be played with before they become doctrine.
experiments.labProtocolTitle: Browse experiments as prompts, not blog posts
experiments.labProtocolDescription: The experiments archive is intentionally tighter and faster: short prompts, clear entry points, and direct jumps into the idea.

emptyState.description: Create the first entry for {section} from the admin panel and it will appear here automatically.

entryDetail.readingNoteBody: This entry belongs to the {section} archive and is managed from the admin control room. New posts for this section automatically appear on the homepage and archive page once published.

cookie.body: This site uses essential cookies for secure admin access and may rely on hosting-level technical cookies or logs to keep the experience stable and safe. By continuing to browse, you acknowledge that use. Read more in the

translatePrompt.question: Read this in {language}?
translatePrompt.error: Couldn't translate this article. Try again later.
